name: Calamaria Gemista (Stuffed Squid)
recipeIngredient: 3/4 cup Dry white or red wine
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash and clean the squid.
2. Grasp the head just below the eyes, pull it off from the rest of the body, and set it aside.
3. Cut away the thin purplish membrane on the outside of the tail section.
4. Using your index finger, scoop out and discard the guts and thin cartilage "icicle" on the inside of the tail section.
5. Rinse tail sections inside and out and set aside in a colander to drain.
6. Take the head section in one hand and apply pressure with your thumb and forefinger around the mouth and eyes, squeezing them out. Discard mouth and eyes.
7. Chop the squid tentacles and have them ready for use in the stuffing.
8. In a large skillet, heat half the olive oil and sauté onion until wilted.
9. Add rice, tentacles, and pine nuts to the skillet and sauté over medium-low heat for 2-3 minutes.
10. Add garlic and currants to the rice mixture and stir quickly with a wooden spoon.
11. Pour in 1/4 cup wine and 1/4 cup water. Season with salt and pepper.
12. Reduce heat to low and simmer, covered, until liquid is almost completely absorbed and rice is soft but only about half cooked, about 15 minutes.
13. About 5 minutes before removing the skillet from the heat, stir in chopped parsley, dill, and mint.
14. Remove the squid and let it cool enough to handle.
15. Using a small teaspoon or butter knife, carefully fill about three quarters of each squid with the rice mixture.
16. Secure the openings with toothpicks.
17. Pour remaining olive oil into a large stewing pot.
18. Place the squid carefully in the pot.
19. Pour in the remaining wine and enough water to cover.
20. Bring to a boil, reduce heat to low, and simmer, covered for about 1-1/2 hours, or until rice is cooked and squid is fork-tender.
21. Twenty minutes before removing the squid from the heat, add chopped tomatoes to the pot and adjust seasoning with salt and pepper.
22. Check throughout cooking to ensure that more water is added so that the mixture doesn't dry out.
23. Serve squid hot or cold with a simple green salad.
SERVING SUGGESTIONS: This dish pairs well with a delicate wine such as a Robola Callinga or Santorini Boutari. Serve with wild greens (horta), village salad (horiatiki salata), and olive bread.
